channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Boeing bows out of Navy’s new trainer jet competition | Breaking Defense
item.description: Boeing said the T-7A Red Hawk training jet they are producing for the Air Force doesn’t fulfill Navy requirements.

item.title: 🌏 Poland intends to buy two more squads of F-35s, minister says | Breaking Defense
item.description: Two extra squadrons of F-35s would double the existing order, from 32 to 64.

item.title: 🕵️ Better collaboration needed on SOCOM programs, says government watchdog | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We found that these [civilian] officials can’t effectively carry out the office’s oversight responsibilities as they can’t always get access to relevant program information or meetings,” GAO said while listing three recommendations.

item.title: 🏛️ Four U-2S Spy Planes Would Be Restored In Bill That Would Save The Dragon Lady Fleet | The War Zone
item.description: Congress is moving again to block the Air Force from retiring the U-2 amid continued questions about capability gaps. The post Four U-2S Spy Planes Would Be Restored In Bill That Would Save The Dragon Lady Fleet appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army commissions second cohort of tech executives into innovation unit | Defense News
item.description: This week, the Army added three more technology executives to Detachment 201, a unit intended to bridge the gap between the commercial sector and military.

item.title: 🔐 Push for new Cyber Force service branch narrowly fails in the Senate | Defense One
item.description: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand’s amendment aimed to place a new service under the Army.

item.title: 🕵️ Seven charts that will define France’s G7 summit | Atlantic Council
item.description: As world leaders gather in Évian-les-Bains for the Group of Seven summit, issues from energy to artificial intelligence will be on the agenda. The post Seven charts that will define France’s G7 summit appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Elon Musk’s Self-Contradictory Military Policy | Foreign Policy
item.description: The soon-to-be trillionaire’s politics undermine his own business as a government contractor.

item.title: 📍 Conflicts are jolting energy systems. Will they cause ‘structural breaks’? | Atlantic Council
item.description: At the 2026 Atlantic Council Global Energy Forum, policy experts shared lessons learned from ongoing wars and explored potential future energy outcomes. The post Conflicts are jolting energy systems. Will they cause ‘structural breaks’? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s defense AI chief predicts ‘new paradigm’ of warfare | Defense News
item.description: “The system that possesses more data and better understands that data, proposes solutions — that system will gain the advantage over the other."

item.title: 🏴 So what’s the strategy for NATO’s transformation? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Host Matthew Kroenig is joined by Admiral Pierre Vandier, Supreme Allied Commander of NATO’s Allied Command Transformation (ACT) to discuss how NATO is confronting the changing nature of war and the evolving threats facing the alliance. NATO ACT leads the Alliance’s military…

item.title: 🌍 Israeli startup unveils laser system it says can kill drones in seconds | Breaking Defense
item.description: “It’s a game changer. It’s smaller, uses only 4kWh, can be connected to a vehicle, and is three to four times less expensive than current laser or microwave systems,” Erex Riahi, the CEO of Esh-Tech, said.

item.title: 🌏 Missile Defense Worked Against Iran. It Might Not Work Against China. | Foreign Policy
item.description: The ongoing war has revealed vulnerabilities that could prove far more dangerous in a conflict over Taiwan.

item.title: 📍 Going Dark: Why Dismantling America’s Ocean Sensors Is a Security Risk | Just Security
item.description: Shutting off ocean observation will reduce the United States' ability to see and respond to developments in fast-changing seas. The post Going Dark: Why Dismantling America’s Ocean Sensors Is a Security Risk appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Don’t Invite Syria Back Into Lebanon | Foreign Policy
item.description: Washington is contemplating the one thing that could rally Lebanese around Hezbollah.

item.title: 🌍 Blockade and Article 2(4) of the UN Charter | Just Security
item.description: "﻿T﻿he U.S. military’s enforcement of the ongoing naval blockade of Iran may have now resulted in fresh violations of the U.N. Charter against four entirely different countries." The post Blockade and Article 2(4) of the UN Charter appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 US plans major cut to jets, warships for NATO operations in Europe, NYT reports | Defense News
item.description: The United States reportedly plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe.

item.title: 🌍 Making Syria’s Transitional Justice Process Meaningful for Survivors and Communities | Just Security
item.description: One of the central questions facing Syria is whether its emerging justice system can earn the trust of those in whose name it is being built. The post Making Syria’s Transitional Justice Process Meaningful for Survivors and Communities appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Humvee maker AM General to pitch unmanned ground vehicle at Eurosatory trade show | Defense News
item.description: The Ukrainian military aims to contract 25,000 unmanned ground platforms in the first half of this year, more than doubling its 2025 total.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 12, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE “We ended the war in Iran today,” President Trump said yesterday at a telerally for the Georgia…

item.title: 🛡️ Charting the Future of the Indonesian Military’s Involvement in Counterterrorism | The Diplomat
item.description: While TNI’s past counterterrorism involvement has proved beneficial, it is important not to overlook how a further expansion of its role could complicate existing efforts.

item.title: 🔐 ‘The threat is there’: Germany to pair P-8s with MQ-9 drones to keep an eye on Russian subs | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The MQ-9B will be the first [unmanned] system we operate with it — the idea is to have those working closely with the aircraft, but step by step,” said Capt. Broder Nielsen, head of the German Naval Aviation Command.

item.title: 📍 Lipsky cited in Reuters on how the G7 agenda is being design to please Trump | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Lipsky cited in Reuters on how the G7 agenda is being design to please Trump appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Tran cited in European Policy Center on the size of the euro denominated international transactions | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read full article here. The post Tran cited in European Policy Center on the size of the euro denominated international transactions appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 PHOTOS: The sights of Berlin Air Show 2026 on Day 3 | Breaking Defense
item.description: A selection of photos from the Breaking Defense team on the ground in Berlin.

item.title: 🏴 How Russia Could Escalate | Foreign Policy
item.description: With progress in Ukraine grinding to a halt, Moscow could seek disruption off the battlefield.

item.title: 📍 Paris defense show may be last chance to buy before war, CEO says | Defense News
item.description: Surging defense spending means Eurosatory is bigger than ever this year, with the number of exhibitors rising 30% to more than 2,600, per the organizer.

item.title: 🏛️ Leonardo in ‘advanced’ talks to expand Italian Army’s AW249 combat helicopter fleet | Breaking Defense
item.description: One of the use-cases envisaged for the platform could be to “fly at high-speed and low-level in order to penetrate deep into enemy territory,” Marco Marinoni, head of the AH-249 acquisition program in the Italian Army, said.

item.title: 📍 Heading Off: New Technique Helps Track Grain Smuggling Expansion to Libya | Bellingcat
item.description: On February 15, 2026, the bulk carrier, Grumant (IMO: 9385879) was pictured at the occupied Ukrainian Port of Feodosia on the Crimean peninsula. Satellite imagery suggests it had already been there for several days. It appeared to stock up on grain before departing on a…

item.title: 🔐 As Cambodia Cracks Down, Cyberscam Networks Test Sri Lanka | The Diplomat
item.description: What happens next will determine whether the island is a temporary refuge for foreign cybercriminals or becomes part of Asia’s cybercrime infrastructure.
